text: Doctor ne phir stress bol kar nikaal diya, par unhe kya pata ki 70 saal ki umar mein bedridden pati ki seva aur ghar ka sara kaam mere hi kandhon par hai. Bacche shehar mein hain aur unhe lagta hai ye sab mera 'farz' hai, isliye main thakan mein bhi nahi ruki kyunki mere rukkne se unki tabiyat aur kharab ho jayegi. Mujhe bas endurance dikhani hai kyunki mere paas koi aur sahara nahi bacha.

Pati ko dementia hai aur wo poora din mujhe pareshan karte hain, par Doctor kehte hain ki mera BP bas 'stress' ki wajah se hai. Main afford nahi kar sakti ki bimar ho kar let jaaun kyunki joint family mein log kya kahenge ki budhi saas ne seva karna chhod diya. Thakan se badan toot raha hai par endurance hi mera asli dharma hai kyunki hospital ka kharcha humein hamesha chinta mein rakhta hai.

Jab Doctor ne kaha ki ye sab dimaag ka khel hai, toh mujhe laga unhe mere caregiving burnout ka ehsas hi nahi jahan main khud bimar hoke bhi apne bimar samdhi ki seva karti hoon. Bahu-bete kaam pe jaate hain aur ghar sambhalna meri duty hai, isliye main apna dard chhupa kar bas machine ki tarah lagi rehti hoon. Mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai kyunki agar main ruki toh sab kahenge ki main 'natak' kar rahi hoon.

Bedridden pati ki seva karte-karte meri apni pehchaan khatam ho gayi hai aur Doctor meri takleef ko bas 'stress' samajhte hain. Is generational gap mein bacche meri loneliness nahi samajhte aur mujhe lagta hai ki rona mardangi ya aurat ki 'izzat' ke khilaf hai, isliye main bas bina ruke seva karti hoon. Main thak kar choor hoon par endurance hi meri zindagi ki sacchai hai kyunki parivaar ki shaan mujh par tiki hai.

Doctor ne phir stress bol kar dismiss kar diya, par unhe nahi dikhta ki ek buzurg aurat ke liye support system na hona kitna bhari padta hai. Dementia se jujh rahe pati ki seva mera farz hai aur main afford nahi kar sakti ki apni health pe dhyan doon kyunki log humein ignore karte hain. Main bas chalti ja rahi hoon kyunki rukkne ka matlab hai apne pati ko laawarish chhod dena, aur ye mere sanskaro ke khilaf hai.
